compiled_state.title: Roadmap skill: persist captured design intent to design-notes.md
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
Context: The /dso:roadmap skill reads design-notes.md for project archetypes and golden paths but never writes captured design intent back to the file. When a non-engineer runs /dso:roadmap immediately after the bootstrap installer, design-notes.md contains template stubs (vision: 'TBD', archetypes: []). Roadmap captures vision and archetype information during Phase 0 intake but discards it; downstream skills (/dso:brainstorm, /dso:sprint) then operate on stale stubs rather than actual project intent. This epic adds a write-back step to /dso:roadmap Phase 0 that persists captured design intent into design-notes.md.

Approach: Modify /dso:roadmap SKILL.md to add a Phase 0 write-back step immediately after intake completes. Stub detection rule (authoritative definition): a field value is a stub if it matches '' (empty string), 'TBD', 'TODO', 'PLACEHOLDER', null, or [] (empty array). Non-stub values are preserved unchanged. Also update check-onboarding.sh to evaluate design field content against the stub detection rule (not just file existence). Cross-skill schema dependency: design-notes.md schema is co-owned by /dso:onboarding; any future onboarding field additions must be reflected in the write-back logic — document this coupling in plugins/dso/docs/SCHEMA-COUPLING.md.

Success Criteria:
1. After a roadmap session where the user provides a vision statement, design-notes.md is updated with the captured vision value; for projects with no UI component (no src/app/ or pages/ directory present), the archetypes field is not required and may remain at its existing value.
2. A non-stub value already present in design-notes.md (any value not matching the stub detection rule) is not overwritten when roadmap runs the write-back step.
3. A stub value (matching the stub detection rule: '', 'TBD', 'TODO', 'PLACEHOLDER', null, or []) in design-notes.md is replaced with the roadmap-captured value after Phase 0 completes.
4. check-onboarding.sh is updated (as part of this epic) to evaluate design fields for non-stub content — a design-notes.md with all stub values returns design_onboarding.pass == false; a design-notes.md with real values (as written by roadmap write-back) returns design_onboarding.pass == true.
5. A new integration test at tests/integration/test_roadmap_writeback.sh exists and passes green; the test: (a) simulates a roadmap Phase 0 session capturing a vision value by invoking the write-back step directly with a fixture input (vision string + archetype list) bypassing interactive intake, (b) asserts design-notes.md is written with that value, (c) asserts a pre-existing non-stub value is not overwritten, (d) asserts a stub value is overwritten; this test is written RED-first before any implementation.

Scenario Analysis (5 surviving red team scenarios):
- Stub detection false positive on intentionally short values (medium, runtime): project acronym 'TBD' classified as stub and overwritten
- Write-back fails silently when design-notes.md is read-only (high, runtime): must surface as hard stop, not silent continue
- Schema mismatch on bootstrapped repos with different field layout (medium, deployment): renamed/nested fields cause silent no-op merge
- Single-element stub array bypasses empty-array rule (medium, configuration): archetypes: ['TBD'] is non-empty, preserved as real content
- Multi-line vision string breaks YAML serialization (high, configuration): must use YAML-aware serialization, not string interpolation

Prompt Alignment: LLM-specific risks — value hallucination (LLM fills stubs with plausible-but-wrong values; SKILL.md must anchor each written value to intake transcript); over-eager stub detection (TODO-as-note values must be protected by non-empty sentence guard). Add read-back verification step after write.

Dependencies: 'DSO NextJS Starter: one-command bootstrap for non-engineer prototyping' (primary epic) must complete first — it establishes the stub-initialized design-notes.md in the installed template. check-onboarding.sh content update is scoped into this epic (not a prerequisite).

Priority: Value=3 (Measurable — removes re-prompt friction for non-engineer persona), Effort=3 (Moderate — SKILL.md modification + check-onboarding.sh update + integration test) -> P2
